prompt: Draft the opening SMS message to candidate Jordan Reyes about the Senior Software Engineer role at Acme Corp. The role is full-time remote with a US base. We want to ask if Jordan has 5 minutes for a quick screening. Save the SMS body to a file named `sms.txt` in the current working directory.

expectations.text: A file named `sms.txt` exists in the run's outputs directory.
`sms.txt` contains an explicit AI disclosure phrase (e.g. 'automated', 'AI', 'AI-powered') in the first sentence the recipient would read.
`sms.txt` names the hiring company 'Acme Corp' (the employer, not just 'Array HQ' the platform).
`sms.txt` includes the SMS opt-out instruction — specifically the literal phrase 'Reply STOP' (or a near-equivalent like 'STOP to opt out').
`sms.txt` addresses the recipient by first name 'Jordan' rather than a full name + ID or generic salutation.

prompt: Draft the first-touch email to a procurement contact at MapleHealth Vendors regarding our upcoming benefits broker review for Acme Corp's 2026 plan year. The contact is Priya Nair (priya@maplehealth.example). We want to introduce ourselves and request a 30-minute intro call next week. Save the full email (subject + body, including any required footer) to a file named `email.txt` in the current working directory.

expectations.text: A file named `email.txt` exists in the run's outputs directory.
`email.txt` discloses the AI nature of the message in the opening line (before the ask), using a phrase like 'AI assistant', 'automated', or equivalent.
`email.txt` identifies 'Acme Corp' as the hiring company on whose behalf the message is sent.
`email.txt` includes a footer (separated from the body by a blank line, '---', or equivalent visual break) stating that the message was generated by an AI assistant on behalf of Acme Corp.
`email.txt` includes an opt-out / unsubscribe instruction (e.g. 'reply UNSUBSCRIBE', 'unsubscribe', 'stop further messages').

prompt: You are continuing an existing SMS thread with candidate Jordan Reyes. The most recent message in this thread (sent yesterday by us) was: 'Hi Jordan — this is an automated screening assistant for Acme Corp, powered by Array HQ. We'd like 5 minutes of your time for a quick screening on the Senior Software Engineer role. Reply STOP to opt out.' Jordan replied 'sure'. Now draft your next SMS asking the first screening question: how many years of professional Python experience do they have? Save your reply to a file named `sms-reply.txt` in the current working directory.

expectations.text: A file named `sms-reply.txt` exists in the run's outputs directory.
`sms-reply.txt` asks Jordan how many years of professional Python experience he has (the literal screening question, in any reasonable phrasing).

`sms-reply.txt` does NOT re-state the AI disclosure phrase from the prior message — i.e. it does not contain 'automated screening assistant', 'powered by Array HQ', or any equivalent fresh AI-disclosure boilerplate. The thread is already disclosed; restating the disclosure on every turn is incorrect.
`sms-reply.txt` does NOT repeat the 'Reply STOP to opt out' opt-out instruction. The opt-out is given once at thread start and is not repeated on every reply.
